disclaimer: Pre-alpha mock signer only — not production MPC. dWallet keys, trust model, and signing protocol are not final.
summary: Both curves (Curve25519+EdDSA, Secp256k1+ECDSA-Keccak256) produce signatures that CRYPTOGRAPHICALLY verify against their dWallet public keys, and the Polet kill switch aborts the lifecycle before Sign when a revoke lands mid-flight — all proven end-to-end on devnet.

coverage.acceptanceCriterion: #5 — Approved USDC -> SUI intent produces a real MessageApproval with status=Signed, signature_len=64, and a valid Ed25519 signature verifiable against the dWallet public key.
#6 — Approved USDC -> ETH intent produces a real Secp256k1 signature verifiable with hash_scheme = Keccak256 against the dWallet public key.

notes: Official @ika.xyz/pre-alpha-solana-client requestDKG() is hardcoded to Curve25519; this script submits the DKG request via raw BCS over gRPC so the curve variant can be set to Secp256k1.
Presign uses global Presign (signature_algorithm=ECDSASecp256k1); PresignForDWallet is only for imported ECDSA dWallets.

coverage.acceptanceCriterion: #9 — Session revoke mid-flight aborts downstream Sign or broadcast without leaking policy thresholds; SDK/frontend surface a distinct session-revoked-midflight state.

result.reason: Session revoked midflight (pre-sign); lifecycle aborted before Sign request.

coverage.acceptanceCriterion: #13 (partial) — New tests + existing tests pass. Proxy build must be clean.

detail.proxyTests: 210/211 pass (the single failure is an unrelated pre-existing DCA test)
